title: Trump Pulled in at Least $2.2 Billion in 2025, Financial Disclosure Shows
summary: The release of a mandatory financial disclosure for 2025 shows that the Trump family’s holdings, particularly the president’s crypto businesses, were stunningly lucrative.

title: Trump’s Moneymaking Run: Unrivaled in Presidential History
summary: The president’s move to open new business ventures, rather than eliminate potential conflicts, defies a long-held tradition.

title: The Supreme Court’s Momentous Term, and How Trump Has Made at Least $2.2 Billion in Office
summary: Plus, zombies at the mall.

title: Control of the Senate Is Up for Grabs, Times/Siena Polls Find
summary: Republicans are defending seats in Alaska, Iowa, Maine, North Carolina, Ohio and Texas as they try to maintain their majority. Democrats are competitive in all six states — but not leading in enough to take the chamber.

title: Takeaways From the Colorado Primary Elections: Another Win for Democratic Socialists
summary: A democratic socialist ousted a veteran congresswoman in Denver, and a U.S. senator lost his bid for governor. But the state’s other senator fended off a progressive primary challenger.

title: Left-Wing Insurgent Ousts 15-Term Congresswoman in Colorado
summary: Melat Kiros, a 29-year-old democratic socialist, unseated Representative Diana DeGette in a Democratic primary to represent the Denver area.

title: Ohio and Iowa Governors Races Tight, New Polls Show
summary: Voters polled in the states, Iowa and Ohio, also voiced high levels of disapproval for President Trump’s performance.

title: Despite Some Losses for Trump, Supreme Court Delivers Enduring Conservative Wins
summary: The justices pushed back on some of President Trump’s signature moves, but they also expanded presidential power and supplied victories on long-sought conservative goals.

title: The Birthright Decision Was Surprisingly Close, Some Legal Scholars Say
summary: A bare majority of Supreme Court justices ruled that President Trump’s executive order was unconstitutional, reflecting a conservative shift on the issue.

title: Takeaways From a Transformative Supreme Court Term
summary: The justices rejected some of President Trump’s signature initiatives, but delivered lasting, long-sought conservative wins.

title: Heat Wave Trudges East on Wednesday
summary: More than 160 million people are under extreme heat warnings or heat advisories ahead of the Fourth of July weekend.

title: With Lessons From a Perilous Blizzard, Mamdani Prepares for Extreme Heat
summary: After more than two dozen people died during a brutal cold snap in New York City, city officials came up with a plan to keep people safe in the cold and extreme heat.

title: 5 Temperature Records Meteorologists Are Watching This Week
summary: New York could record 100 degrees for the first time since 2012, as a brutal heat wave spreads east.

title: Underground Births, Under Bombardment: Childbearing in Ukraine
summary: Bombings, blackouts and displacement compound maternal anxiety. Still, many women persevere. “We must bring new life,” one said.

title: Back in Congress, Tom Kean Jr. Faces a New Challenge: Staying There
summary: The New Jersey Republican has explained the reason for his long absence: depression. Now he must convince voters that he deserves another term.

title: What Tom Kean Did and Didn’t Explain About His Absence
summary: Representative Thomas Kean Jr., a New Jersey Republican, has finally reappeared in Congress. Questions remain about his nearly four-month absence from public life, which he said was due to depression.

title: For Many Americans, Depression Is Familiar. Here’s What We Know.
summary: Representative Thomas Kean Jr. announced he had been hospitalized for depression. More than one in four U.S. adults report having been diagnosed with the condition.

title: Trump Suggestion of a Syrian Crackdown on Hezbollah Confounds Many in Mideast
summary: During peace talks, President Trump repeatedly floated the idea that Syria could help subdue Hezbollah in Lebanon. The proposal revived bitter memories.

title: U.S. and Iran Set to Hold Indirect Talks After Trading Attacks: What to Know
summary: Negotiators are in Qatar for indirect talks and ship traffic has risen in the Strait of Hormuz, even as Tehran and Washington remain far apart on key issues. Here’s what to know.

title: U.S. Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Most Powerful A.I. Models
summary: The move allows Anthropic to bring its most powerful technologies back online, de-escalating a feud with the Trump administration.

title: Fact-Checking Trump’s Inaccurate Accounts of U.S. History
summary: We fact-checked the president’s colorful — and inaccurate — accounts of American history from the Battle of Gettysburg to the Unabomber.

title: Victor Willis, Lead Singer of the Village People, Dies at 74
summary: Often performing dressed as a helmeted police officer, he also co-wrote the group’s biggest hits, including “Y.M.C.A.” and “Macho Man.”

title: The Dress Code for Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding? Expect a Black-Tie Event.
summary: New details have emerged about the celebration at Madison Square Garden, widely believed to be a wedding, including a dress code.

title: A Risky Burial in the Heart of an Ebola Outbreak
summary: Safe interment is critical to containing the deadly disease, but in recent days around one Congolese town, that was not always being done.

title: After Venezuela Earthquakes, Deportees from the U.S. Are Missing or Found Dead
summary: Many of the 146 Venezuelans deported from the United States the day the earthquake struck are feared dead.

title: U.S. Mobilizes for Venezuela Despite Trump’s Disdain for Foreign Aid
summary: The State Department has promised $100 million in new funds to aid groups, after President Trump was criticized for an anemic response to an earthquake in Myanmar last year.

title: After a Tap Heard in Rubble, U.S. Rescue Teams Begin a Grueling Marathon
summary: Surrounded by the devastation of Venezuela’s earthquakes, emergency specialists from California, Virginia and Florida work with locals to search for survivors.

title: Five Words That Shook the World
summary: It wasn’t Thomas Jefferson who made “All men are created equal” actually mean something.

title: Is Kidmaxxing the Ultimate Status Symbol for Ultimate Wealth?
summary: What drives these men to reproduce at an industrial scale?

title: A Separation of Powers
summary: We look at the Supreme Court rulings on birthright citizenship and transgender athletes.

title: What Older Adults Should Know About GLP-1 Drugs as Medicare Coverage Expands
summary: A Medicare pilot beginning Wednesday will broaden access to medications like Wegovy and Zepbound. There are unique risks for those over 65.
